name: Environments
summary: An Environments object is a collection of Environment objects. It is used to manage the environments in a model. An Environment object represents an environment in the model. Environments are used to control the background and lighting of the model. Environments can be used as skyboxes, for reflections, and to link the sun to the environment.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieve an Sketchup::Environment by name.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieve an Sketchup::Environment by name.

methods.name: add
methods.summary: Note: The supported file formats are HDR, EXR and SKE.
methods.remarks: Note: The supported file formats are HDR, EXR and SKE. The #add method adds an Sketchup::Environment to the Sketchup::Environments.

params.name: path
params.doc: the path to the image or SKE file used for the environment

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The #add_observer method is used to add an observer to the environments collection.
methods.remarks: The #add_observer method is used to add an observer to the environments collection.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate over all the environments in the Sketchup::Environments.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate over all the environments in the Sketchup::Environments.

methods.name: purge_unused
methods.summary: The #purge_unused method is used to remove unused environments.
methods.remarks: The #purge_unused method is used to remove unused environments.

methods.name: remove
methods.summary: The #remove method removes an Sketchup::Environment from the Sketchup::Environments.
methods.remarks: The #remove method removes an Sketchup::Environment from the Sketchup::Environments.

returns.doc: true if the environment was removed, false if it was not found

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The #remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The #remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: size
methods.signature: size => Integer Also known as: length
methods.summary: The #size method retrieves the number of environments in the Sketchup::Environments.
methods.remarks: The #size method retrieves the number of environments in the Sketchup::Environments.

properties.name: current
properties.summary: The #current method is used to get the current environment in the Sketchup::Environments.
properties.remarks: The #current method is used to get the current environment in the Sketchup::Environments.
